info.title: Agentic AI Platform - Ingestion Service API
info.description:
## Overview

Ingestion Service is designed to be used as part of Trimble Agentic AI Platform to ingest documents to the Knowledge Service.
It exposes a simple API to process documents and ingest them in to the Knowledge Service libraries.

## Getting Started

**PRIOR TO USING THIS API, YOU MUST AGREE TO OUR TERMS OF SERVICE**

The API is protected by OAuth2.0 and requires a valid TID access token in the `Authorization` header. Refer to [TID Authentication Guide](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/authentication) for details.

### Required TID Token Scopes

The Knowledge Service API requires the following TID token scopes:

- `openid` - Get user identity
- `kb-ingest` - Your application must be subscribed to the Ingestion Service API product using [Trimble Cloud Console](https://console.trimble.com/)

## Capabilities Overview

- Ingest a file into a library via URL
- Ingest a file into a library via upload
- Monitoring the ingestion job status and error message if the job failed
- List recent ingestion jobs
- Get an ingestion by ID

## Common API Concepts

This API uses HTTPS requests with JSON-formatted content in requests and responses.

### Cross-Origin Resource Sharing

This API features Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) implemented in compliance with the
[W3C spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/cors/) that allows cross-domain communication from the browser.
All responses have a wildcard same-origin which makes them completely public and accessible to everyone,
including any code on any site.

### Date Format

All dates in the API use UTC and are strings in the [ISO 8601 combined date and time representation
format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Combined_date_and_time_representations)
Example `2015-05-15T15:50:38.311Z`.

### Eventual Read Consistency

The API responses are eventually consistent.
When you read data from the service, the response might not reflect the results of the most recently completed
write operation. The response might include some stale data due to caching
in place. If you repeat your read request after a short time, the response should return the latest data.

### Paginated Responses

All endpoints that return collection of resources follow the same schema for query parameters and
response structure to paginate the results.
Pagination allows to limit the size of the large responses and aimed to improve the responsiveness and overall
performance when fetching large result sets. The overall latency is improved
by parallelization of data processing and transfer.

Pagination is cursor-based and client-driven. The requests accept `skipToken` and `pageSize` query parameters.

If the `skipToken` query parameter is not specified, first page of the result set is returned.

The `pageSize` parameter informs the server on how many items client
wants to receive in a single page at most. Server may return less number of items than requested if requested response payload is too large or if some of the items are not accessible to the requester.
If `pageSize` parameter is not specified then server return maximum possible number of items by default.

The maximum page size is different for each endpoint and specified as part of the endpoint description.
It may also vary depending on the request parameters (e.g. `fields` query parameter).
If the `pageSize` parameter is greater than specified limit endpoint can support server will return error to help client avoid bug in pagination implementation.

The `links.next.href` property in the response contains the URL to the next page of results. If there are no more results, the `links.next.href` property is not present in the response.
Server may return response with `links.next.href` property even if the response contains no / lesser than requested(pageSize) items.
Server may return less items than `pageSize` requested (or even zero). It could happen e.g. if resources requested are too large to fit into a single response
or if some (or all `pageSize` requested) resources are not accessible to the requester. In this case requester is expected to call `links.next.href` to fetch the rest of the resources,if `links.next.href` is present in the response.
In extreme cases response could be empty with the `links.next.href` present, it means that all resources on the current page is not accessible to the requester.

### Common Query Parameters

In addition to query parameters (`pageSize`, `skipToken`) used for paging responses there are several query
parameters that are commonly used across all APIs.

- the `sortBy` query parameter is used to specify the order of the items in the response. If not provided, the default order is applied (typically creation timestamp). The `sortBy` parameter is a string that can be one of the predefined values, e.g. `createdAt`, `-createdAt`. The `-` and `+` prefixes are used to specify the order direction (ascending or descending). Default order is ascending if no prefix is specified.

- all identifiers are represented as strings, client should not make assumptions about the format of the identifiers, they are opaque to the client
- all APIs return `x-request-id` header that contains a unique identifier for the request. This identifier can be used to track the request in the logs and for debugging purposes.

## Data Model

The API is built around the `job` concept. A job runs asynchronously and represents a state machine that goes though a series of states.
The state status of the job can be monitored by polling the job descriptor by the job identifier.
The current job state is represent by the `status` property:

- `UPLOADABLE` - waiting for the content to be uploaded
- `QUEUED` - content is available, waiting for the parsing to start
- `PARSING` - parsing of the content into the text is in progress
- `PARSED` - parsing completed, waiting for the chunking to start
- `CHUNKING` - chunking phase is in progress
- `COMPLETED` - final job state in case of success
- `FAILED` - final job state in case of any error

## Data Retention

All information related to jobs is retained only for the duration of 7 days from the job creation and deleted after that.

## Access Control Model

Only job creator has access to the job information.

## Error Handling

### Handling Errors and Retry Logic

Common HTTP Response Status Codes are used.
The response body for all error responses are in the same format and include the error code and message.

When you send requests to and get responses from the Service API, you might encounter two types of API errors

- **Client errors** - Client errors are indicated by a 4xx HTTP response code. Client errors indicate
that the service found a problem with the client request. This can include such things as an authentication
failure or missing required parameters. Fix the issue in the client application before submitting the request again.
- **Server errors** - Server errors are indicated by a 5xx HTTP response code and need to be resolved by Trimble.
You may resubmit/retry the request until it succeeds.

For each API error, the API Gateway returns the following values

- A status code, for example, `400`
- An error type, for example, `tag:ValidationException`
- An error message, for example, `Supplied AttributeValue is empty, must contain exactly one of the
supported data types`

### Error Response Body Schema

All error responses follow the same structure for the response body and are conformant to [RFC 9457](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html) specification.
The error types use `tag:` URI schema and cannot be dereferenced.

| Error type                                | HTTP Status Code | Error description |
| ----------------------------------------- | --- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `tag:BadGateway`                          | 502 | Bad gateway error.|
| `tag:ChunkError`                          | 500 | Generic chunking error.|
| `tag:ChunkerContentValidationFailed`      | 400 | Document content validation failed during chunking.|
| `tag:ChunkerInitializationFailed`         | 500 | Failed to initialize the document chunker.|
| `tag:ChunkerSizeLimitExceeded`            | 400 | Document size exceeds the maximum limit for chunking.|
| `tag:ChunkerStrategyNotSupported`         | 400 | The requested chunking strategy is not supported.|
| `tag:Conflict`                            | 409 | The resource already exists.|
| `tag:ConflictingHeaders`                  | 400 | Only one of If-Match or If-None-Match header is allowed in a request.|
| `tag:DatabaseOperationFailed`             | 500 | Database operation failed.|
| `tag:DocumentAnalysisFailed`              | 500 | Failed to analyze the document.|
| `tag:DocumentChunkingFailed`              | 500 | Failed to chunk the document content.|
| `tag:DocumentContentExtractionFailed`     | 500 | Failed to extract content from the document.|
| `tag:DocumentDownloadFailed`              | 500 | Failed to download the document from storage.|
| `tag:DocumentParsingFailed`               | 500 | Failed to parse the document content.|
| `tag:DocumentUploadFailed`                | 500 | Failed to upload the document to storage.|
| `tag:HttpError`                           | 500 | Generic HTTP error.|
| `tag:InternalServerError`                 | 500 | The internal server error, please try again later.|
| `tag:InvalidAcl`                          | 400 | The ACL cannot be empty on space level.|
| `tag:InvalidIfMatchHeader`                | 400 | Invalid If-Match Header.|
| `tag:InvalidName`                         | 400 | The resource name is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidPageSize`                     | 400 | The request contains invalid page size.|
| `tag:InvalidParam`                        | 400 | Please provide a valid version.|
| `tag:InvalidParentParam`                  | 400 | The parent param is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidPayload`                      | 400 | One of id or name is mandatory. In case of a new file upload name is mandatory.|
| `tag:InvalidQueryParam`                   | 400 | Invalid query param: X.|
| `tag:InvalidSkipToken`                    | 400 | The request contains invalid skip token.|
| `tag:InvalidSortParameter`                | 400 | The given sort parameter is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidStateParam`                   | 400 | The state param is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidUpload`                       | 400 | The file status is not UPLOADABLE.|
| `tag:InvalidUrl`                          | 404 | The url is not valid.|
| `tag:InvalidVersion`                      | 412 | Specified version is not the latest version.|
| `tag:KbChunkUploadFailed`                 | 500 | Failed to upload chunks to the knowledge base.|
| `tag:KbDocumentCreationFailed`            | 500 | Failed to create document in the knowledge base.|
| `tag:KbError`                             | 500 | Generic knowledge base error.|
| `tag:KbGenerationCreationFailed`          | 500 | Failed to create generation in the knowledge base.|
| `tag:KbGenerationPublishFailed`           | 500 | Failed to publish generation in the knowledge base.|
| `tag:KbServiceAuthenticationFailed`       | 401 | Authentication failed with the knowledge base service.|
| `tag:KbServiceConnectionFailed`           | 503 | Failed to connect to the knowledge base service.|
| `tag:MethodNotAllowed`                    | 405 | Method is not allowed on this resource.|
| `tag:NotAcceptableMediaType`              | 406 | The requested media type is not supported.|
| `tag:PageSizeExceeded`                    | 400 | The pageSize parameter exceeds the maximum supported page size (N).|
| `tag:ParseError`                          | 500 | Generic parsing error.|
| `tag:ParserContentValidationFailed`       | 400 | Document content validation failed during parsing.|
| `tag:ParserInitializationFailed`          | 500 | Failed to initialize the document parser.|
| `tag:ParserStrategyNotSupported`          | 400 | The requested parsing strategy is not supported.|
| `tag:PermissionDenied`                    | 403 | Permission denied for the requested resource.|
| `tag:QueuePublishError`                   | 500 | Queue publish error, please try again later.|
| `tag:ResourceNotFound`                    | 404 | The file or folder not found.|
| `tag:ServiceUnavailable`                  | 503 | The service is currently unavailable.|
| `tag:SourceUriMissing`                    | 400 | The source URI is missing from the request.|
| `tag:StaleObject`                         | 400 | Cannot update as request contains stale data.|
| `tag:TokenExchangeError`                  | 400 | Failed to change the TID token to communicate with downstream service.|
| `tag:UnAuthorized`                        | 401 | The request is not authorized.|
| `tag:UnsafeUrl`                           | 400 | The download URL is not safe to fetch.|
| `tag:UnprocessableContent`                | 422 | The request is not valid.|

### Error Status Codes (Client and Server Errors)

HTTP status codes indicate whether an operation is successful or not.
A response code of 2xx indicates that the operation was successful. Other error codes indicate either a
client error (4xx) or a server error (5xx).
The error table lists the errors returned by the service. Some errors are resolved if you retry the same request.
As a general rule, the timeout errors, network errors, throttling errors, and 5xx errors may be retried.
The 401 error code usually indicates expired credentials and should be retried after refreshing the access token.
The error table indicates which errors are likely to be resolved with successive retries.

If the value of the Retry column is

- **Yes** - Submit the same request again.
- **Yes if idempotent** - Submit the same request again, but only if the given method has been
implemented with idempotence.
- **No** - Fix the problem on the client-side before submitting a new request.

For more information about retrying requests, see Error Retries and Exponential Backoff.

| HTTP Status Code | Error type class                      | Retry                                             |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| 400              |  Bad Request Exception                | No                                                |
| 401              |  Unauthorized Exception               | No, could be retried after acquiring a new token  |
| 403              |  Access Denied Exception              | No                                                |
| 404              |  Not Found Exception                  | No                                                |
| 406              |  Not Acceptable Exception             | No                                                |
| 409              |  Conflict Exception                   | No                                                |
| 429              |  Too Many Requests Exception          | Yes                                               |
| 500              |  Internal Service Failure Exception   | Yes                                               |
| 502              |  Bad Gateway Exception                | Yes if idempotent                                 |
| 503              |  Service Unavailable Exception        | Yes                                               |
| 504              |  Endpoint Request Timed-out Exception | Yes if idempotent                                 |

### Catching Errors

For your application to run smoothly, you need to build in logic to catch and respond to errors.
One typical approach is to implement your request within a try block or if-then statement.
The client-side SDKs typically perform retries and error checking out of the box.

The error code and description are always send in the request response body in addition to HTTP status code.
To troubleshoot the problem please capture the request identifier using the `x-request-id`, `X-Azure-Ref`, `x-amzn-RequestId` HTTP response headers. Inform the value received in these headers to support team.

### Error Retries and Exponential Backoff

Numerous components on a network, such as DNS servers, switches, load balancers,
and others can generate errors anywhere in the life of a given request.

The usual technique for dealing with these error responses in a networked environment
is to implement retries in the client application.
This technique increases the reliability of the application and reduces operational costs for the developer.

If you're not using the official SDKs, you should retry original requests that receive server errors (5xx).
However, client errors (4xx, other than a TooManyRequestsException) indicate you need to
revise the request itself to correct the problem before trying again.

In addition to simple retries, it is recommended to use an exponential backoff algorithm for better flow control.
The idea behind exponential backoff is to use progressively longer waits between retries
for consecutive error responses.
For example, you might let one second elapse
before the first retry, four seconds before the second retry, 16 seconds before the third retry, and so on.
However, if the request has not succeeded after a minute, the problem might be a hard limit and not the
request rate.
For example, you may have reached the maximum number of pipelines allowed. Set the maximum
number of retries to stop around one minute.

## Payload compression

The service accepts `gzip` and `br` compressed request payloads. The `Content-Encoding` header must be sent in the request
to decode the request payload properly.

The service supports the following content encodings for responses:

- `gzip`
- `br`
- `identity`

Only responses larger than 1KB might be encoded. Small responses are sent without encoding.

The encoding (compression) method can be negotiated with the `Accept-Encoding` header.
The following `Accept-Encoding` header formats, according to the [RFC 7231](<https://tools.ietf.org/html/>
rfc7231#section-5.3.4) specification, are supported:

- `Accept-Encoding:br,gzip`
- `Accept-Encoding:` - If the Accept-Encoding header is present and empty, only the unencoded content is acceptable.
- `Accept-Encoding:*` - This is the default value if the header is not present. This directive does not suggest that any algorithm is supported but indicates that no preference is expressed. Service will decide on the best compression method.
- `Accept-Encoding:br;q=0.5,gzip=1.0`
- `Accept-Encoding:gzip;q=1.0,identity;q=0.5,*;q=0`
If the header is not set, the default value is `*` as defined in RFC 7231. In such cases, the payload is compressed with best supported compression method.

## Supported File Formats

The service supports a wide range of file formats, automatically selecting the appropriate parsing strategy based on the file type.

### Complex Documents (using Document Intelligence)

These formats are parsed using Azure Document Intelligence for advanced layout and content extraction:

| File Format | Extensions | MIME Types |
|-------------|------------|------------|
| PDF | `.pdf` | `application/pdf` |
| Microsoft Word | `.docx` | `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` |
| Microsoft Excel | `.xlsx` | `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet` |
| Microsoft PowerPoint | `.pptx` | `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation` |

### Text-Based Documents (using Plain Text Parser)

These formats are processed as plain text, preserving original formatting and structure:

| Category | Extensions | Description |
|----------|------------|-------------|
| Text Files | `.txt`, `.log`, `.md`, `.markdown`, `.rst`, `.adoc`, `.asciidoc` | Plain text and markup documents |
| Data Files | `.csv`, `.tsv`, `.json`, `.xml`, `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.sql` | Structured data formats |
| Web Files | `.html`, `.htm`, `.css`, `.less`, `.sass`, `.scss` | Web markup and stylesheets |
| Programming Languages | `.py`, `.js`, `.jsx`, `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.java`, `.c`, `.cpp`, `.cs`, `.go`, `.rb`, `.php`, `.swift`, `.kt`, `.rs`, `.scala`, `.lua`, `.r`, `.m` | Source code files |
| Scripts | `.sh`, `.bash`, `.bat`, `.ps1`, `.fish`, `.zsh` | Shell and script files |
| Configuration | `.ini`, `.conf`, `.cfg`, `.properties`, `.env`, `.lock`, `.manifest`, `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, `.dockerignore`, `.editorconfig`, `.htaccess`, `.htpasswd`, `.robots`, `.sitemap` | Configuration and settings files |
| Other | `.tex`, `.bib`, `.vim`, `.emacs` | LaTeX, bibliography, and editor files |

**Note:** The service automatically detects the file type based on both the file extension and MIME type to select the optimal parsing strategy.

## Limits

The service has the following limits:

- Maximum job duration: 1 hour

post.description: Creates a new job. A document is always created in the Knowledge Base as part of job creation. If document creation fails, the job will not be created. If `downloadUrl` is provided, the job will be queued for processing immediately. Otherwise `uploadUrl` will be returned back and you are expected to upload the content to the provided URL.

schema.description: Order of the items in the response. The order is applied before the pagination. If not provided, the default order is applied: by creation timestamp descending (newest jobs first).

parameters.description: Order of the items in the response. The order is applied before the pagination. If not provided, the default order is applied: by creation timestamp descending (newest jobs first).

get.description: Get the information about the caller

parameters.name: fields

schema.description: Fields to be included in the response. If not provided, minimal number of fields will be included as default.
schema.title: Fields

parameters.description: Fields to be included in the response. If not provided, minimal number of fields will be included as default.

strategy.description: The strategy to use for chunking.

codeLanguage.description: The programming language of the content. **Required** when `strategy` is `code` (unless using `auto` which can detect). If not provided and the `strategy` is `code` or `auto`, system will try to detect the language from the filename. If detection fails, `python` will be used as default. **Ignored** when `strategy` is `text`, `json`, or `openapi`.

chunkSize.title: Chunksize
chunkSize.description: The maximum size of each chunk in tokens. Must be greater than `chunk_overlap`.

chunkOverlap.title: Chunkoverlap
chunkOverlap.description: The overlap between consecutive chunks in tokens. Helps maintain context across chunk boundaries. Must be less than `chunk_size`.

separators.title: Separators
separators.description:
The separators to use for chunking the content.

**Applicable strategies:** `auto`, `text`, `code`
**Ignored strategies:** `json`, `openapi`

**Text strategy defaults** (if not provided):
  - `\n\n` (double newline)
  - `\n` (newline)
  - ` ` (space)
  - `""` (empty string)
  - `.` (period)

**Code strategy:** Separators are automatically determined from `code_language`. Custom separators are **ignored**.

**How it works:** Uses recursive character text splitter. Attempts to split on the first separator, then second, and so on, until the target `chunk_size` is reached.

ChunkerOptions.title: ChunkerOptions
ChunkerOptions.description: Chunker options that defines how the content should be chunked.

ChunkingStrategy.title: ChunkingStrategy
ChunkingStrategy.description:
Defines the chunking algorithm used to split document content into chunks.

- `text`: Uses recursive character text splitting with configurable separators.
  Default separators (if not provided): `["\n\n", "\n", " ", "", "."]`.
  Respects `chunkSize` and `chunkOverlap` settings.

- `code`: Uses language-aware splitting optimized for source code.
  Separators are automatically determined from `codeLanguage`
  (custom separators ignored).
  If `codeLanguage` is not provided, attempts to detect from filename;
  defaults to Python.
  Respects `chunkSize` and `chunkOverlap` settings.

- `json`: Uses recursive JSON splitting that preserves JSON structure.
  The `separators` field is ignored. If individual JSON chunks exceed
  10,000 characters, they are further split using the text chunker.
  Note: `chunkOverlap` is not used for the JSON splitter
  (only for text fallback).
  Content must be valid JSON.

- `openapi`: Specialized chunking for OpenAPI 2.0/3.0 specifications.
  Creates one chunk per API path/operation and one chunk per component
  schema. Output chunks are YAML-formatted. Examples are removed to
  reduce size. Custom `separators`, `chunkSize`, and `chunkOverlap`
  are largely ignored.
  Content must be a valid OpenAPI specification (JSON or YAML).

- `auto` (default): Automatically detects the optimal strategy based on:
  1. Content-type header (e.g., `application/openapi+json` -> openapi,
     `application/json` -> json)
  2. File extension (e.g., `.py` -> code with Python, `.json` -> json)
  3. Document category (code, data, markup, text, or complex)
  For code files, the programming language is auto-detected from the
  filename.

kbUrl.title: Kburl
kbUrl.description: The base URL of the Knowledge Base service to ingest document to. Must be a naked URL, i.e. without any path, query parameters or fragments.If not provided, the default KB service URL will be used.

libraryId.title: Libraryid
libraryId.description: The Knowledge Base Library ID. The content will be ingested to this library. Library must exist. You must have sufficient permissions to ingest the content to the library.

documentId.title: Documentid
documentId.description: The ID of the document to ingest. A document is always created in the Knowledge Base during job creation. If the document creation fails, the job will not be created. If provided and document does not exist, a new document will be created with the provided ID. If provided and document exists, a new generation will be created for the document. If not provided, a new document will be created with a random ID.

source.description: Information about the document source. These attributes will be set on a document in the Knowledge base when document is ingested.

downloadUrl.title: Downloadurl
downloadUrl.description: The URL to download the document content.  Must be public URL or self-signed. If not provided, you are expected to upload the content to the uploadUrl returned in the response.

parserOptions.description: Comprehensive parsing options that abstract different parsing frameworks. If not provided, default parsing options will be used.

chunkerOptions.description: Options for configuring the chunking process. If not provided, default chunking options will be used.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Optional list of tags to associate with the document. If not provided for a new document, defaults to an empty list. If not provided for an existing document, the current tags are preserved.

CreateJobRequest.title: CreateJobRequest
CreateJobRequest.description: Request model for creating a new job with comprehensive parsing options.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: The URI of the source document. If not provided, the download URL will be used.If download URL is also not provided, the source URI will be set to 'tag:unknown'.

updatedAt.title: Updatedat
updatedAt.description: Last time source document was changed

updatedBy.title: Updatedby
updatedBy.description: The actor who changed the source document last time

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the source document in bytes. If not provided, it will be calculated after the document is uploaded.

retryInfo.description: Information about retry attempts, including current attempt,max attempts, and whether retryable

ErrorInfo.title: ErrorInfo
ErrorInfo.description: Detailed error information for job processing failures.

href.title: Href
href.description: A URL could be absolute of relative to the service base path.

id.title: Id
id.description: The globally unique job identifier (random identifier assigned by the service on job creation)

kbUrl.title: Kburl
kbUrl.description: The base URL of the Knowledge Base service document is ingested to. This is a naked URL, i.e. without any path, query parameters or fragments.

libraryId.title: Libraryid
libraryId.description: The Knowledge Base Library ID. The content will be ingested to this library. You must have sufficient permissions to ingest the content to the library.

documentId.title: Documentid
documentId.description: The ID of the document in the Knowledge Base. A document is always created during job creation, so this field is always present. The chunks will be uploaded as a new generation to this document.

updatedAt.title: Updatedat
updatedAt.description: Last time job descriptor was changed

source.description: Information about the document source. These attributes will be set on a document in the Knowledge base when document is ingested.

downloadUrl.title: Downloadurl
downloadUrl.description: The URL to download the document content.  Must be public URL or self-signed. If not provided, you are expected to upload the content to the uploadUrl returned in the response.

uploadUrl.title: Uploadurl
uploadUrl.description: Pre-signed URL to upload contents of the file

parserOptions.description: Comprehensive parsing options that abstract different parsing frameworks. If not provided, default parsing options will be used.

chunkerOptions.description: Options for configuring the chunking process. If not provided, default chunking options will be used.

error.description: Detailed error information if the job failed. This field is populated only when the job status is FAILED.

first.description: A URL that will retrieve the first page of results. Always present even if result set is empty.

next.description: A URL that will retrieve the last page of results. If there are no more pages, this is omitted.

LinksResponse.title: LinksResponse
LinksResponse.description:
Response model for links.

The `links.next.href` property in the response contains the URL
to the next page of results.
If there are no more results, the `links.next.href` property is not present
in the response.
Server may return response with `links.next.href` property even if the response
contains no / lesser than requested(pageSize) items.

strategy.description: The parsing strategy to use. The `auto` strategy will choose the parsing framework automatically based on the document type and content.

language.title: Language
language.description: Language code for text recognition and analysis (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'es').

locale.title: Locale
locale.description: Locale hint for text recognition and document analysis.

modelId.title: Modelid
modelId.description: The Document Intelligence model ID to use for analysis.

useOcr.title: Useocr
useOcr.description: Whether to use Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for text extraction.

ocrConfidenceThreshold.title: Ocrconfidencethreshold
